title: nepeta nepetella subsp. murcica
intro: Nepeta nepetella subsp. murcica, also known as Catmint or Spanish Catmint, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the mint family Lamiaceae. It is native to the Iberian Peninsula and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to soothe wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues. The health benefits of Nepeta nepetella subsp. murcica include its ability to calm the nervous system, reduce anxiety and stress, and promote a good night's sleep. The plant can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be made by steeping the leaves and flowers in hot water or mixing with other herbs to create a medicinal remedy.
